ol.li: Predicate pushdown: Filters applied before joins reduce intermediate sizes
Join ordering: Starts with smallest filtered tables (products: 100 rows)
Avoids cartesian explosion: Never joins 5M × 1K unfiltered Key Transformation:

ul.li: Naive: (c ⋈ o) ⋈ oi) ⋈ p — grows to 5M rows before filtering
Optimized: ((p_filtered ⋈ oi) ⋈ o_filtered) ⋈ c — stays under 500K throughout

div.p: Join: order_items ⋈ products Input: 5M × 1K rows Est. Output: 500K rows Cost: 2,100,000
Join: orders ⋈ order_items Input: 1M × 5M rows Est. Output: 5M rows Cost: 1,500,000
Join: customers ⋈ orders Input: 10K × 1M rows Est. Output: 1M rows Cost: 800,000
HashJoin: filtered_p ⋈ filtered_oi Input: 100 × 500K rows Est. Output: 50K rows Cost: 15,000
HashJoin: filtered_o ⋈ customers Input: 50K × 10K rows Est. Output: 50K rows Cost: 8,000
